root_motivation: The gossip.md coordination channel between /dream and /build must remain a single coherent log regardless of which machine (laptop or work node) appended to it — right now the log silently forks the moment the user moves to the work node, corrupting the shared narration of the wintermute system.
user_persona: Joe Yen, running wintermute daemons on a two-machine fleet (laptop + work node); uses /dream and /build daily and expects gossip.md to be the single coordination channel across both machines.

unfakeable_metric.name: acceptance_tests_passing
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | awk '{print $6}' | head -1

acceptance_criteria.description: Appending a new block to a fixture gossip.md causes wm-tether-gossip to publish exactly one wm.fleet.gossip.append event whose body equals the appended block and whose seq is previous_seq + 1, asserted against a captured publish sink.

An incoming wm.fleet.gossip.append event from node 'worknode' is appended to the local fixture file under a (via tether) provenance header; the file's prior content is byte-for-byte unchanged above the new block (append-only invariant).
Dedup: the same (node, seq) event delivered twice is applied exactly once (the local file gains one block, not two).
Loop guard: a block this node published is NOT re-applied to its own local file when the same event arrives back over the bus (no self-echo duplication).
seq persists across restarts: after a restart, the next published event's seq continues from the persisted value, not from zero.

(deferred) A round-trip selftest publishes a tagged block locally, confirms it arrives on wm.fleet.gossip.append exactly once, and (in a two-instance harness) is applied to the second instance's file exactly once.
cargo test green; sigpipe::reset() first in main() (grep-asserted); wm-tether-gossip status | head does not panic.

scope: Tail gossip.md for new appends and publish them as wm.fleet.gossip.append NATS events
Subscribe to wm.fleet.gossip.append and apply remote-node events as provenance-headed appends
Deduplicate by (node, seq) so redelivered events are idempotent
Loop-guard against self-echo via node-origin check
sigpipe::reset() as first line of main()
non_goals: Total ordering across nodes — cross-node interleaving is timestamp-ordered best-effort only
Rewriting or reordering existing gossip.md history — append-only contract is absolute
Conflict resolution or merge — gossip.md is append-only; there is nothing to merge
Supporting more than one file (gossip.md only in v1)
Providing the NATS bus or tether-link itself — depends on existing wm-busbridge

additional.append_only_invariant: gossip.md lines are never reordered or deleted by this tool

five_whys_trace.q: Why does gossip.md need to be mirrored?
five_whys_trace.a: Because /dream and /build both write to and read from gossip.md as a single coordination channel, but the file only exists on one machine.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does having the file only on one machine matter?
five_whys_trace.a: When the user is at the work node, /build and /dream can't see the laptop's gossip, so they start a parallel silent divergence — the brain's self-narration splits in two without anyone noticing.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is the silent split harmful?
five_whys_trace.a: The gossip log is the shared working memory of the wintermute system — PRDs are seeded from it, build progress is logged to it, the user reads it to understand what the system is doing. A split log is two systems lying to each other.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't we just use a shared NFS or git repo for gossip.md?
five_whys_trace.a: The bus (wm-busbridge / NATS) is already the fleet's synchronization primitive; using a different mechanism (NFS, git push) would add a dependency outside the established fleet architecture and break the append-only contract when git merge creates a merge commit.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the append-only contract matter so strictly?
five_whys_trace.a: The skills treat gossip.md as an audit trail — they never rewrite lines they wrote before, so the log is implicitly trusted to be causal and complete. Any reordering would corrupt that trust and cause skills to make decisions based on wrong history.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should AC6 (round-trip embedded NATS selftest) be deferred?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Yes — PRD explicitly marks it deferred_acs: [6]. A mock test in tests/mocks/ac6.rs verifies the same public API surface without a real NATS server.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Which NATS client? async-nats vs nats?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: async-nats (the official, actively maintained async client) for the real daemon path; for tests, use a channel-pair mock so tests don't need a NATS server.
